title: transport.rs: connection_manager emits Reconnecting after relay drop but not after clean relay-initiated close
description: In connection_manager (transport.rs line 201), after a successful connection is established and the relay then disconnects (relay_disconnect=true), the code falls through to the reconnect backoff loop and sends ClientEvent::Reconnecting. This is correct. However, a relay-initiated clean close (Message::Close) also sets relay_disconnect=true and sends Reconnecting — which is correct. The issue is that after the relay_disconnect path, Reconnecting is sent unconditionally even when the caller has already dropped in_tx (is_closed check on line 291 only runs AFTER the Reconnecting send attempt). The is_closed check happens too late: the code checks in_tx.is_closed() but then the Reconnecting send already handles the is_err() case. This is benign but the is_closed() check on line 291 is therefore dead code — every early-exit case is already handled by the send().is_err() return. Not a correctness bug but a readability/maintenance hazard: a future contributor adding logic between the is_closed check and the send will be confused.
